seoTitle: Jisemi Products | Automotive Ethernet PHYs, SerDes & Interface ICs | LiTong
seoDescription: Jisemi product catalog featuring automotive Ethernet PHYs, high-speed SerDes solutions, and industrial connectivity ICs for automotive and industrial applications.

faqs.question: What product categories does Jisemi offer?
faqs.answer: Jisemi offers four main product categories: Automotive Ethernet PHYs supporting 100Mbps to 10Gbps data rates with AEC-Q100 qualification; High-Speed SerDes solutions for camera, display, and sensor interfaces; Industrial Ethernet products for factory automation and robotics; and Multi-Protocol Interface ICs supporting various connectivity standards. All products are designed for high-reliability applications in automotive and industrial environments.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our product categories to explore Jisemi's complete portfolio, or contact our FAE team for product recommendations based on your specific connectivity requirements.

faqs.question: How do I select the right Jisemi Ethernet PHY for my automotive application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right Jisemi Ethernet PHY depends on bandwidth requirements, network topology, and functional safety needs. For basic infotainment and diagnostics, 100BASE-T1 (100 Mbps) is typically sufficient. For ADAS and camera systems, 1000BASE-T1 (1 Gbps) provides adequate bandwidth. For autonomous driving and high-resolution sensor fusion, MultiGBASE-T1 (2.5/5/10 Gbps) is recommended. Consider AEC-Q100 grade based on operating temperature range and ASIL requirements for safety-critical applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Define your bandwidth requirements first, then select the appropriate speed grade. Contact our FAE team for detailed selection guidance.

faqs.question: What is the difference between automotive-grade and industrial-grade Jisemi products?
faqs.answer: Automotive-grade Jisemi products are AEC-Q100 qualified with enhanced reliability testing, wider temperature ranges (-40°C to +125°C), and support for functional safety (ISO 26262). They undergo additional qualification tests including high-temperature operating life and temperature cycling. Industrial-grade products are qualified for -40°C to +85°C operation and meet industrial reliability standards. Both grades offer excellent performance, but automotive-grade is required for vehicle applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select automotive-grade for vehicle applications requiring AEC-Q100. Choose industrial-grade for factory automation and general industrial use.

faqs.question: Does Jisemi offer pin-compatible alternatives to other Ethernet PHY suppliers?
faqs.answer: Yes, many Jisemi Ethernet PHYs are designed to be pin-compatible with popular industry-standard products, enabling easy migration without PCB redesign. This includes compatibility with major automotive Ethernet suppliers. Pin compatibility reduces design risk and accelerates time-to-market when transitioning to Jisemi solutions. Contact our FAE team for specific cross-reference information and migration support.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team with your current part numbers to identify pin-compatible Jisemi alternatives and receive migration support.

faqs.question: What package options are available for Jisemi products?
faqs.answer: Jisemi products are available in various automotive-qualified packages including QFN, eQFN, and BGA options. Package selection depends on thermal requirements, PCB space constraints, and manufacturing processes. QFN packages offer excellent thermal performance and are widely used for automotive Ethernet PHYs. BGA packages provide higher pin density for complex multi-port devices. All packages meet automotive reliability requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review product datasheets for available packages, or contact our FAE team for package selection guidance based on your thermal and space requirements.

categories.name: Automotive Ethernet PHYs
categories.description: Jisemi automotive Ethernet PHYs deliver high-speed connectivity for ADAS, autonomous driving, and in-vehicle networking applications. These devices support IEEE 802.3 standards with AEC-Q100 qualification.
categories.longDescription: Jisemi automotive Ethernet PHY distributor selection guide: Our comprehensive portfolio includes 100BASE-T1 (100 Mbps) PHYs for basic connectivity, 1000BASE-T1 (1 Gbps) PHYs for ADAS and camera systems, and MultiGBASE-T1 (2.5/5/10 Gbps) PHYs for autonomous driving and high-bandwidth applications. All PHYs are AEC-Q100 qualified and support automotive temperature ranges (-40°C to +125°C). The products feature advanced diagnostic capabilities, low power consumption, and robust EMC performance. Key selection criteria include data rate requirements, functional safety needs (ASIL level), temperature range, and package type. These products enable next-generation automotive architectures including zonal E/E architectures and software-defined vehicles.

selectionGuide.title: Automotive Ethernet PHY Selection Guide
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting the right Jisemi automotive Ethernet PHY based on bandwidth, safety, and application requirements.

faqs.question: What Ethernet standards does Jisemi automotive PHYs support?
faqs.answer: Jisemi automotive Ethernet PHYs support IEEE 802.3 standards including 100BASE-T1 (IEEE 802.3bw), 1000BASE-T1 (IEEE 802.3bp), and MultiGBASE-T1 (IEEE 802.3ch) for 2.5/5/10 Gbps operation. These standards are specifically designed for automotive applications with single-pair Ethernet (SPE) technology, reducing cabling weight and cost while providing robust performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose 100BASE-T1 for basic connectivity, 1000BASE-T1 for ADAS, MultiGBASE-T1 for autonomous driving based on bandwidth needs.

faqs.question: How do I select the appropriate data rate for my application?
faqs.answer: Data rate selection depends on application bandwidth requirements. 100BASE-T1 (100 Mbps) is suitable for diagnostics, body electronics, and basic infotainment. 1000BASE-T1 (1 Gbps) supports camera systems, ADAS, and high-resolution displays. MultiGBASE-T1 (2.5/5/10 Gbps) is required for autonomous driving sensor fusion, high-resolution camera arrays, and backbone networks. Consider future expansion needs when selecting data rates.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate your bandwidth requirements including overhead, then select the next higher standard speed to provide margin.

faqs.question: What functional safety support does Jisemi provide?
faqs.answer: Jisemi automotive Ethernet PHYs support ISO 26262 functional safety with ASIL-B to ASIL-D capability depending on the device. The products include safety mechanisms such as built-in self-test (BIST), error detection, and diagnostic coverage. Jisemi provides safety manuals, FMEDA analysis, and technical support for safety-critical system development.
faqs.decisionGuide: Determine your ASIL requirements early in the design phase and select PHYs with appropriate safety support.

faqs.question: Are Jisemi automotive PHYs compliant with OPEN Alliance standards?
faqs.answer: Yes, Jisemi automotive Ethernet PHYs are compliant with OPEN Alliance TC1, TC8, and TC12 specifications. These specifications define interoperability requirements, EMC test methods, and conformance tests for automotive Ethernet. Compliance ensures compatibility with other OPEN Alliance certified devices and systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify OPEN Alliance compliance for interoperability with other automotive Ethernet devices in your network.

faqs.question: What diagnostic capabilities do Jisemi automotive PHYs offer?
faqs.answer: Jisemi automotive Ethernet PHYs provide comprehensive diagnostic capabilities including cable fault detection, link quality monitoring, temperature sensing, and voltage monitoring. Advanced devices support IEEE 802.3bw/bp/ch diagnostic features and provide detailed status registers for system monitoring and predictive maintenance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select PHYs with appropriate diagnostic features based on your system monitoring and maintenance requirements.

products.name: 100BASE-T1 Automotive Ethernet PHY
products.shortDescription: AEC-Q100 qualified 100Mbps automotive Ethernet PHY with low power consumption and advanced diagnostics for body elect...
descriptionParagraphs: The JL1001 is a high-performance 100BASE-T1 automotive Ethernet PHY compliant with IEEE 802.3bw standard. It provides reliable 100 Mbps connectivity over single unshielded twisted pair (UTP) cable for automotive applications.

Featuring AEC-Q100 Grade 1 qualification (-40°C to +125°C), the JL1001 is ideal for body electronics, diagnostics, and infotainment systems. The device includes advanced diagnostic features for cable fault detection and link quality monitoring.

With ultra-low power consumption of less than 100mW in active mode and robust EMC performance, the JL1001 enables efficient and reliable in-vehicle networking. The device supports various MAC interfaces including MII, RMII, and RGMII.

faeReview.author: David Zhang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Automotive Connectivity
faeReview.content: The JL1001 is an excellent choice for entry-level automotive Ethernet applications. In my experience supporting body electronics designs, the low power consumption and robust EMC performance make it ideal for distributed modules. The cable diagnostics feature has helped customers identify wiring issues during production testing. I particularly appreciate the flexible MAC interface options which simplify integration with various microcontrollers. The AEC-Q100 Grade 1 qualification ensures reliable operation in harsh automotive environments.
faeReview.highlight: Low power and robust EMC make it ideal for distributed body electronics

faqs.question: What is the maximum cable length supported by JL1001?
faqs.answer: The JL1001 supports up to 15 meters of single unshielded twisted pair (UTP) cable at 100 Mbps, which is sufficient for most in-vehicle networking applications. The actual maximum length depends on cable quality and environmental conditions. The device includes adaptive equalization to optimize performance across various cable lengths and conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications requiring longer cable runs, consider using higher quality cable or adding signal conditioning.

faqs.question: How do I configure the MAC interface on JL1001?
faqs.answer: The JL1001 supports MII, RMII, and RGMII MAC interfaces selectable through strap pins or register configuration. The default interface mode is determined by strap pins at power-up, while register configuration allows dynamic interface selection. Refer to the datasheet for detailed strap pin configurations and register settings for each interface mode.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose RMII for reduced pin count, RGMII for higher performance, MII for legacy compatibility.

faqs.question: What is the power consumption in sleep mode?
faqs.answer: The JL1001 features multiple power modes. In active mode, power consumption is less than 100mW. In standby mode, consumption drops to approximately 10mW while maintaining link detection. Deep sleep mode reduces consumption to less than 1mW, with wake-on-LAN capability for remote wake-up. Transition times between power modes are typically less than 10ms.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use deep sleep mode for battery-powered applications, standby for quick wake-up requirements.

faqs.question: Does JL1001 support cable diagnostics?
faqs.answer: Yes, the JL1001 includes comprehensive cable diagnostic features including open/short detection, cable length estimation, and link quality monitoring. These diagnostics can be accessed through MDIO registers and help identify wiring issues during production testing and field maintenance. The device can detect cable faults with 1-meter resolution.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use cable diagnostics during production testing and for troubleshooting field issues.

faqs.question: What EMC performance can I expect from JL1001?
faqs.answer: The JL1001 is designed for robust EMC performance meeting automotive requirements. It complies with CISPR 25 Class 5 for emissions and ISO 11452/ISO 7637 for immunity. The device includes integrated filtering and slew rate control to minimize EMI. Actual system EMC performance depends on PCB layout and cable routing - follow the layout guidelines in the datasheet for optimal results.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow recommended PCB layout guidelines and use proper filtering for best EMC performance.

products.name: 1000BASE-T1 Automotive Ethernet PHY
products.shortDescription: AEC-Q100 qualified 1Gbps automotive Ethernet PHY supporting IEEE 802.3bp with ASIL-B functional safety for ADAS and c...
descriptionParagraphs: The JL5001 is a high-performance 1000BASE-T1 automotive Ethernet PHY compliant with IEEE 802.3bp standard. It delivers 1 Gbps connectivity over single unshielded twisted pair cable, enabling high-bandwidth applications in modern vehicles.

With AEC-Q100 Grade 1 qualification and ASIL-B functional safety support, the JL5001 is ideal for ADAS, camera systems, and high-resolution display applications. The device features advanced signal processing for robust operation over automotive cables.

The JL5001 supports various MAC interfaces including RGMII and SGMII, with comprehensive diagnostic capabilities for production testing and field maintenance. Low latency and deterministic performance make it suitable for real-time applications.

faeReview.author: Sarah Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - ADAS Applications
faeReview.content: The JL5001 has been my go-to recommendation for ADAS camera and display connectivity. The 1 Gbps bandwidth easily handles multiple high-resolution camera streams, while the ASIL-B safety support meets requirements for safety-critical applications. I've seen excellent results in camera module designs with minimal signal integrity issues. The RGMII interface provides straightforward integration with automotive processors. The diagnostic features have helped customers achieve high manufacturing yield by detecting cable issues early.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent signal integrity and ASIL-B support for ADAS camera applications

faqs.question: What is the latency of JL5001?
faqs.answer: The JL5001 features low latency design with typical end-to-end latency of less than 2 microseconds. This includes both transmit and receive paths. The deterministic latency makes it suitable for real-time applications such as ADAS sensor processing. Latency remains consistent across temperature and voltage variations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify latency requirements for your real-time application; JL5001 is suitable for most ADAS use cases.

faqs.question: How does JL5001 handle EMC in high-speed operation?
faqs.answer: The JL5001 includes advanced EMC features for 1 Gbps operation including programmable slew rate control, output amplitude adjustment, and integrated filtering. These features allow optimization of EMC performance for specific cable and layout conditions. The device meets CISPR 25 Class 5 emissions and ISO 11452 immunity requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use programmable EMC features to optimize for your specific cable and layout conditions.

faqs.answer: JL5001 features industry-leading power efficiency with multiple power modes for optimized energy consumption. faqs.decisionGuide: Review power specifications in datasheet for your operating conditions.

categories.name: High-Speed SerDes
categories.description: Jisemi high-speed SerDes solutions enable reliable transmission of video, sensor, and data signals over single coaxial or STP cables for automotive and industrial applications.
categories.longDescription: Jisemi high-speed SerDes distributor selection guide: Our comprehensive SerDes portfolio includes solutions for camera, display, and sensor connectivity in automotive and industrial systems. Products support data rates from 2 Gbps to 12 Gbps, enabling transmission of high-resolution video and sensor data over cost-effective cabling. All SerDes products are AEC-Q100 qualified for automotive applications and support industrial temperature ranges. Key features include forward channel data transmission, reverse channel control, and power-over-coax capability. The products support various protocols including MIPI CSI-2, MIPI DSI, and proprietary formats. Selection criteria include data rate requirements, cable type (coaxial or STP), power delivery needs, and functional safety requirements.

selectionGuide.title: SerDes Selection Guide
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting the right Jisemi SerDes solution based on data rate, protocol, and application requirements.

faqs.question: What protocols do Jisemi SerDes support?
faqs.answer: Jisemi SerDes solutions support various protocols including MIPI CSI-2 for camera interfaces, MIPI DSI for display interfaces, and raw data modes for generic sensor connectivity. The devices can transport video, control, and power over a single cable, simplifying system design and reducing wiring complexity.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select SerDes based on your interface protocol requirements; contact FAE for protocol-specific recommendations.

faqs.question: What cable types are supported by Jisemi SerDes?
faqs.answer: Jisemi SerDes support both coaxial cable and shielded twisted pair (STP) cable. Coaxial cable enables longer reach and power-over-coax capability, while STP provides cost-effective solutions for shorter distances. Cable selection depends on reach requirements, power delivery needs, and cost constraints.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use coaxial for longer reach and power delivery; use STP for cost-sensitive short-distance applications.

faqs.question: What are the key features of High-Speed SerDes?
faqs.answer: High-Speed SerDes from Jisemi offer industry-leading performance with automotive-grade reliability, comprehensive diagnostic features, and excellent EMC performance. Products are designed for demanding applications with extended temperature ranges and long-term availability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review product datasheets for detailed specifications and contact FAE for application-specific recommendations.

faqs.question: How do I select the right High-Speed SerDes for my application?
faqs.answer: Selection depends on your specific requirements including data rate, temperature range, safety level, and interface type. Consider future expansion needs and consult the selection guide for detailed recommendations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Define your requirements first, then use the selection guide to identify suitable products.

faqs.question: What support is available for High-Speed SerDes implementation?
faqs.answer: LiTong provides comprehensive support including reference designs, application notes, schematic review, and on-site FAE support. Evaluation boards are available for performance validation. For more detailed information and application-specific recommendations, please refer to the product datasheet or contact our FAE team for technical support.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact FAE early in design cycle for optimal support and access to reference designs.

products.name: 2Gbps Camera SerDes
products.shortDescription: AEC-Q100 qualified 2Gbps SerDes pair for camera applications with MIPI CSI-2 support and power-over-coax capability.
descriptionParagraphs: The JL2001 is a high-performance SerDes pair designed for automotive camera applications. The serializer converts MIPI CSI-2 data to high-speed serial format for transmission over coaxial or STP cable, while the deserializer recovers the data at the receiver end.

Supporting data rates up to 2 Gbps, the JL2001 enables transmission of high-definition video from camera modules to processing units. The integrated power-over-coax feature simplifies camera module power delivery.
With AEC-Q100 Grade 1 qualification and comprehensive diagnostic features, the JL2001 is ideal for surround-view cameras, rear-view cameras, and driver monitoring systems.

faeReview.author: Michael Liu
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Camera Systems
faeReview.content: The JL2001 has been widely adopted in surround-view camera systems due to its reliable performance and ease of integration. The power-over-coax feature significantly simplifies camera module design by eliminating the need for separate power wiring. I've seen excellent video quality even with 15-meter cable runs. The MIPI CSI-2 interface provides straightforward connection to popular image sensors. The diagnostic features help identify cable and connectivity issues during production.
faeReview.highlight: Power-over-coax simplifies camera module design significantly

products.name: 4Gbps High-Resolution SerDes
products.shortDescription: High-performance 4Gbps SerDes supporting 4K video transmission with advanced equalization for long cable reach.
descriptionParagraphs: The JL4001 is a high-performance SerDes pair designed for high-resolution camera and display applications. Supporting data rates up to 4 Gbps, it enables transmission of 4K video streams over automotive-grade cabling.

Featuring advanced adaptive equalization and pre-emphasis, the JL4001 maintains signal integrity over cable lengths up to 15 meters. The device supports both MIPI CSI-2 for cameras and MIPI DSI for displays.
With comprehensive diagnostic capabilities and AEC-Q100 qualification, the JL4001 is suitable for next-generation ADAS and autonomous driving systems requiring high-resolution sensor input.

faeReview.author: Jennifer Wang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - High-Resolution Systems
faeReview.content: The JL4001 enables true 4K video transmission in automotive environments, which is essential for next-generation ADAS. The adaptive equalization automatically compensates for cable variations, ensuring reliable operation across production tolerances. I've successfully used this in designs with multiple 4K cameras for surround-view systems. The power-over-coax capability reduces cabling complexity significantly.
faeReview.highlight: 4K support with adaptive equalization for reliable long-reach transmission

categories.name: Industrial Ethernet
categories.description: Jisemi industrial Ethernet solutions provide robust connectivity for factory automation, robotics, and process control applications with real-time performance.
categories.longDescription: Jisemi industrial Ethernet distributor selection guide: Our industrial Ethernet portfolio includes robust PHYs and switches designed for harsh industrial environments. Products support real-time Ethernet protocols including EtherCAT, PROFINET, and standard IEEE 802.3 Ethernet. All industrial products operate over extended temperature ranges (-40°C to +85°C) and provide enhanced EMC performance for noisy industrial environments. Key features include deterministic latency, redundant network support, and comprehensive diagnostics. The products are designed for long-term availability and industrial reliability requirements. Selection criteria include protocol support, port count, temperature range, and real-time performance requirements.

selectionGuide.title: Industrial Ethernet Selection Guide
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting the right Jisemi industrial Ethernet solution based on protocol and application requirements.

faqs.question: What industrial Ethernet protocols does Jisemi support?
faqs.answer: Jisemi industrial Ethernet products support various real-time protocols including EtherCAT, PROFINET IRT, Ethernet/IP, and standard IEEE 802.3 Ethernet. The devices can be configured for different protocols through software settings, providing flexibility for diverse industrial applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select products based on your specific protocol requirements; contact FAE for protocol-specific configuration guidance.

faqs.question: What redundancy features are available?
faqs.answer: Jisemi industrial Ethernet switches support various redundancy protocols including RSTP, MRP, and PRP/HSR for high-availability applications. These features enable fast failover in case of network failures, ensuring continuous operation in critical industrial systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose redundancy features based on your availability requirements; MRP is commonly used in industrial automation.

faqs.question: What are the key features of Industrial Ethernet?
faqs.answer: Industrial Ethernet from Jisemi offer industry-leading performance with automotive-grade reliability, comprehensive diagnostic features, and excellent EMC performance. Products are designed for demanding applications with extended temperature ranges and long-term availability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review product datasheets for detailed specifications and contact FAE for application-specific recommendations.

products.name: Industrial Ethernet PHY
products.shortDescription: Robust industrial Ethernet PHY supporting 10/100/1000 Mbps with enhanced EMC performance for factory automation.
descriptionParagraphs: The JL8001 is a robust industrial Ethernet PHY designed for harsh factory environments. Supporting 10/100/1000 Mbps operation, it provides reliable connectivity for industrial automation equipment.

Featuring enhanced EMC performance and extended temperature range (-40°C to +85°C), the JL8001 operates reliably in noisy industrial environments. The device includes advanced surge protection and isolation features.
With support for various industrial Ethernet protocols and comprehensive diagnostics, the JL8001 is ideal for PLCs, HMIs, and industrial gateways.

faeReview.author: Robert Huang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Industrial Applications
faeReview.content: The JL8001 has proven to be extremely reliable in factory automation applications. The enhanced EMC performance handles the noisy electrical environment typical of manufacturing floors. I've deployed this in numerous PLC and HMI designs with excellent results. The surge protection has saved customers from field failures during electrical transients.
faeReview.highlight: Robust EMC and surge protection for harsh industrial environments

faqs.answer: The JL8001 meets industrial EMC standards including IEC 61000-4 series for immunity and EN 55032 for emissions. The device includes integrated filtering and protection to ensure reliable operation in harsh electromagnetic environments typical of industrial facilities.

products.name: Industrial Ethernet Switch
products.shortDescription: 5-port industrial Ethernet switch with real-time support and redundancy protocols for factory automation.
descriptionParagraphs: The JL8101 is a 5-port industrial Ethernet switch designed for factory automation applications. Supporting 10/100/1000 Mbps on all ports, it enables flexible network configurations.

Featuring support for real-time protocols including EtherCAT and PROFINET, the JL8101 provides deterministic performance for motion control and robotics. Redundancy protocols including RSTP and MRP ensure high availability.
With rugged design, extended temperature range, and comprehensive management features, the JL8101 is ideal for industrial networking applications.

faeReview.author: Lisa Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Industrial Networking
faeReview.content: The JL8101 is an excellent choice for industrial automation networks. The real-time protocol support enables deterministic performance critical for motion control. The redundancy features have helped customers achieve high availability in production lines. The web-based management interface simplifies configuration and monitoring.
faeReview.highlight: Real-time support and redundancy for industrial automation

faqs.question: What redundancy protocols does JL8101 support?
faqs.answer: The JL8101 supports multiple redundancy protocols including RSTP (Rapid Spanning Tree Protocol), MRP (Media Redundancy Protocol), and PRP (Parallel Redundancy Protocol). These protocols enable fast failover in case of network failures, ensuring high availability for critical industrial applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose MRP for ring topologies, RSTP for mesh networks, PRP for highest availability requirements.

categories.name: High-Speed Interface ICs
categories.description: Jisemi multi-protocol high-speed interface ICs provide flexible connectivity solutions supporting various standards for automotive and industrial applications.
categories.longDescription: Jisemi high-speed interface IC distributor selection guide: Our interface IC portfolio includes multi-protocol transceivers and bridge devices supporting various high-speed standards. Products support protocols including PCIe, USB, SATA, and proprietary interfaces with data rates up to 10 Gbps. All interface ICs are available in automotive and industrial grades with appropriate qualification. Key features include protocol conversion, signal conditioning, and flexible configuration options. The products enable system designers to interface between different protocols and standards seamlessly. Selection criteria include supported protocols, data rate, temperature range, and package requirements.

selectionGuide.title: Interface IC Selection Guide
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting the right Jisemi interface IC based on protocol and application requirements.

faqs.question: What protocols do Jisemi interface ICs support?
faqs.answer: Jisemi interface ICs support various high-speed protocols including PCIe Gen1/2/3, USB 3.0/3.1, SATA, and proprietary interfaces. Multi-protocol devices can be configured for different standards through register settings, providing flexibility for diverse applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select interface ICs based on your specific protocol requirements; contact FAE for multi-protocol configuration guidance.

faqs.question: What are the key features of High-Speed Interface ICs?
faqs.answer: High-Speed Interface ICs from Jisemi offer industry-leading performance with automotive-grade reliability, comprehensive diagnostic features, and excellent EMC performance. Products are designed for demanding applications with extended temperature ranges and long-term availability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review product datasheets for detailed specifications and contact FAE for application-specific recommendations.

faqs.question: How do I select the right High-Speed Interface ICs for my application?
faqs.answer: Selection depends on your specific requirements including data rate, temperature range, safety level, and interface type. Consider future expansion needs and consult the selection guide for detailed recommendations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Define your requirements first, then use the selection guide to identify suitable products.

faqs.question: What support is available for High-Speed Interface ICs implementation?
faqs.answer: LiTong provides comprehensive support including reference designs, application notes, schematic review, and on-site FAE support. Evaluation boards are available for performance validation. For more detailed information and application-specific recommendations, please refer to the product datasheet or contact our FAE team for technical support.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact FAE early in design cycle for optimal support and access to reference designs.

faqs.question: What are the typical applications for High-Speed Interface ICs?
faqs.answer: High-Speed Interface ICs are used in various applications including protocol conversion between different standards, signal extension for long trace or cable runs, system bridging to connect different interfaces, and test equipment for high-speed signal analysis. These devices enable flexible system design by supporting multiple protocols and providing signal conditioning capabilities.
faqs.decisionGuide: Identify your interface requirements and select devices that support your specific protocols and data rates.

products.name: Multi-Protocol Interface Bridge
products.shortDescription: Flexible multi-protocol interface bridge supporting PCIe, USB, and SATA with configurable operation modes.
descriptionParagraphs: The JL9001 is a versatile multi-protocol interface bridge designed for protocol conversion and signal extension applications. Supporting PCIe, USB 3.0, and SATA protocols, it enables flexible system connectivity.
Featuring configurable operation modes and comprehensive signal conditioning, the JL9001 maintains signal integrity across various media. The device supports data rates up to 8 Gbps per lane.
With automotive and industrial grade options, the JL9001 is suitable for a wide range of applications from in-vehicle systems to industrial equipment.

faeReview.author: Kevin Zhao
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Interface Solutions
faeReview.content: The JL9001 provides excellent flexibility for system designers needing protocol conversion. The configurable operation modes allow a single device to serve multiple functions, reducing inventory complexity. I've used this successfully in designs requiring PCIe to USB bridging and SATA signal extension. The signal conditioning maintains integrity even with challenging PCB layouts.
faeReview.highlight: Multi-protocol flexibility reduces design complexity and inventory

faqs.question: How do I configure JL9001 for different protocols?
faqs.answer: The JL9001 protocol mode is configured through strap pins at power-up or through register settings during operation. Configuration options include PCIe lane width (x1/x2/x4), USB 3.0 mode, and SATA mode. Refer to the datasheet for detailed configuration settings and timing requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use strap pins for fixed configurations, register settings for dynamic mode switching.

products.name: High-Speed Signal Conditioner
products.shortDescription: Advanced signal conditioner supporting PCIe Gen4 and USB 3.1 with adaptive equalization for long trace and cable appl...
descriptionParagraphs: The JL9101 is an advanced high-speed signal conditioner designed for demanding signal integrity applications. Supporting PCIe Gen4 (16 Gbps) and USB 3.1 (10 Gbps), it enables reliable data transmission over long traces and cables.

Featuring adaptive equalization and advanced signal processing, the JL9101 automatically compensates for channel losses and maintains signal quality. The device supports both redriver and retimer modes of operation.
With comprehensive diagnostic capabilities and automotive qualification options, the JL9101 is ideal for high-performance computing and automotive applications.

faeReview.author: Alex Wu
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - High-Speed Design
faeReview.content: The JL9101's adaptive equalization is impressive - it automatically optimizes for different channel conditions, saving significant tuning time in production. The dual-mode operation (redriver/retimer) provides flexibility for various applications. I've successfully used this in designs with long backplane traces and cable assemblies where signal integrity was critical.
faeReview.highlight: Adaptive equalization automatically optimizes for different channel conditions

alternativeParts.reason: Lower cost option for lower speed requirements

faqs.question: What is the difference between redriver and retimer modes?
faqs.answer: In redriver mode, the JL9101 provides analog equalization and signal boosting without clock and data recovery (CDR). This mode has lower latency but limited ability to clean up jitter. In retimer mode, the device performs CDR, completely regenerating the signal with fresh jitter characteristics. Retimer mode is recommended for longer channels or when jitter cleaning is required.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use redriver mode for short channels and low latency requirements; use retimer mode for long channels and jitter-sensitive applications.
